steps.title: Use the sample size effect
steps.content: For a survey percentage calculated the same way, a larger random sample usually gives a smaller margin of error because the estimate is based on more data. The larger sample reduces the typical sampling variation around the estimate.

steps.title: Apply it to the new sample
steps.content: The sample size increases from $144$ to $288$, so the margin of error is expected to decrease. The point estimate is not forced to move higher or lower just because the sample is larger; it is the precision that changes. The answer is A.
